report_name: Microsoft Uncovers Active Typosquatting Campaign on npm Targeting Developer Credentials

threat_actors.name: vpmdhaj
threat_actors.description: Threat actor who published 14 malicious, typosquatted npm packages to steal developer secrets from cloud and CI/CD environments. The actor used the email address a39155771@gmail.com.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor published 14 malicious, typosquatted packages to the public npm registry, compromising the software supply chain for developers who mistakenly install them.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The campaign uses typosquatting, a form of social engineering, to trick developers into installing malicious packages with names similar to legitimate ones.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript
attack_patterns.description: A malicious `preinstall` script hook in the package.json file was used to automatically execute a JavaScript payload upon installation.

attack_patterns.name: Unsecured Credentials
attack_patterns.description: The malware payload scans the compromised developer environment for sensitive credentials, including private keys stored in locations like `~/.aws/credentials`.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: The malware was designed to harvest a wide range of secrets, including HashiCorp Vault tokens and npm publish tokens, from the developer's environment.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Harvested credentials and secrets were exfiltrated to an attacker-controlled command and control server.

malware.name: npm Typosquatting Malware
malware.description: A collection of 14 malicious npm packages that use a 'preinstall' hook to execute a payload that steals AWS credentials, HashiCorp Vault tokens, and other developer secrets.

tools.name: Bun
tools.description: A legitimate JavaScript runtime downloaded and used by the second-generation malware payload to execute a second-stage script, likely to evade detection.

identities.name: npm
identities.description: The public package registry targeted by the typosquatting campaign.

identities.name: Developers
identities.description: The primary target group for the typosquatting campaign, whose credentials and secrets were stolen.

identities.name: Amazon Web Services (AWS)
identities.description: Provider of cloud services whose credentials were a primary target for theft.

identities.name: HashiCorp
identities.description: Provider of HashiCorp Vault, whose tokens were targeted for theft.

identities.name: OpenSearch
identities.description: A legitimate open-source project whose libraries were impersonated by the malicious typosquatted packages.

identities.name: ElasticSearch
identities.description: A legitimate project whose libraries were impersonated by the malicious typosquatted packages.

infrastructure.name: Attacker C2 Server
infrastructure.description: A command and control server used to receive exfiltrated credentials and secrets from compromised developer environments.

indicators.name: Execution of Bun JavaScript Runtime
indicators.pattern:
title: Execution of Bun JavaScript Runtime
id: 2b8c1f9d-5e6a-4b3c-8a7f-9d1e2f3a4b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of the Bun JavaScript runtime (bun.exe). While Bun is a legitimate tool, its execution in environments where it is not the standard developer runtime can indicate malicious activity, as seen in a recent npm typosquatting campaign.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/05/29/typosquatted-npm-packages-used-to-steal-cloud-and-ci-cd-secrets/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-29
tags:
  - attack.t1059.007
level: medium
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of the Bun runtime by developers.
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\bun.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the Bun JavaScript runtime, which was used in this campaign to execute a second-stage payload.

indicators.name: Suspicious Child Process of npm
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Child Process of npm
id: 8c1d2e3f-4a5b-6c7d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects npm spawning network utility processes or script interpreters, which can be indicative of malicious preinstall/postinstall scripts.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/05/29/typosquatted-npm-packages-used-to-steal-cloud-and-ci-cd-secrets/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-29
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
  - attack.t1059
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\npm.cmd'
      - '\npm.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\wget.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\bun.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects suspicious child processes spawned by npm, which could indicate a malicious `preinstall` hook is executing.

indicators.name: Threat Actor Email vpmdhaj
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Threat Actor Email Address vpmdhaj
id: 4a5b6c7d-8e9f-0a1b-2c3d-4e5f6a7b8c9d
status: experimental
description: Detects the email address associated with the threat actor 'vpmdhaj' in email logs.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/05/29/typosquatted-npm-packages-used-to-steal-cloud-and-ci-cd-secrets/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-29
tags:
  - attack.t1585.002
level: high
logsource:
  category: email
detection:
  selection:
    Sender: 'a39155771@gmail.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the email address associated with the threat actor 'vpmdhaj'.

indicators.name: Malicious Use of Bun Runtime
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Malicious Use of Bun Runtime File
id: 9d1e2f3a-4b5c-6d7e-8f9a-0b1c2d3e4f5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the presence or execution of bun.exe, which was used in a typosquatting campaign to execute malicious payloads.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/05/29/typosquatted-npm-packages-used-to-steal-cloud-and-ci-cd-secrets/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-29
tags:
  - attack.t1059.007
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\bun.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of the `bun.exe` file, which was used to execute malicious payloads in this campaign.
